question_title: Will H.R. 33 (United States–Taiwan Expedited Double-Tax Relief Act) be enacted into law before May 1, 2026?
summary: H.R. 33 passed the House 423-1 on January 15, 2025, and was referred to the Senate Finance Committee the next day. Over 13 months later, there has been zero further action. The companion bill S. 199 was introduced by bipartisan Senate committee chairs but also hasn't moved. The Taiwan tax provisions were not included in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (signed July 4, 2025). With only ~2 months until the May 1, 2026 deadline, the bill would need rapid Senate committee markup, floor passage, and presidential signature — none of which show any signs of being scheduled. The previous version in the 118th Congress also died in the Senate. While theoretically passable by unanimous consent given strong bipartisan support, 13 months of inaction makes this extremely unlikely within the remaining window.

factors.description: Bill has been stuck in Senate Finance Committee since Jan 16, 2025 — over 13 months with zero recorded action on any tracker (GovTrack, LegiScan, BillTrack50)
Only ~2 months remain until May 1, 2026 deadline — insufficient for full Senate process (committee markup, floor vote, presidential signature) absent extraordinary urgency
Previous version (H.R. 5988, 118th Congress) also passed House but died in Senate, per KPMG: 'first introduced in 2023 during the 118th Congressional session but was not passed into law'

Taiwan tax provisions were NOT included in One Big Beautiful Bill Act (reconciliation package signed July 4, 2025) despite being available — Senate did not prioritize it even in a major legislative vehicle
Strong bipartisan support: 423-1 House vote, Senate Finance Chair Crapo and Ranking Member Wyden introduced companion bill S.199, Foreign Relations leadership also sponsors
Unanimous consent passage theoretically possible at any time given bipartisan backing — provides a non-zero fast-track path
Holland & Knight reported internal Senate disagreements about child tax credit linkage complicating the bill's advancement
No news of any Senate scheduling, markup, or floor vote in 2026 — absence of process indicators is informative for a high-profile bill

reasoning:
**Forecast: 4% probability** that H.R. 33 becomes law before May 1, 2026.

**Key reasoning:**

- **13+ months of Senate inaction**: Despite passing the House 423-1 in January 2025 and having bipartisan Senate sponsors (including Finance Committee Chair Crapo), the bill has sat dormant in the Senate Finance Committee with no markup, vote, or scheduling.
- **Tight deadline**: Only ~2 months remain. Full Senate passage plus presidential signature in this window would require extraordinary urgency that shows no signs of materializing.
- **Precedent of failure**: The same bill died in the Senate during the 118th Congress (2023-2024) after also passing the House.
- **Missed the big vehicle**: The Taiwan tax provisions were not included in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (July 2025 reconciliation), the most obvious legislative vehicle.
- **Only path to YES**: Senate unanimous consent — theoretically possible given bipartisan support, but 13 months of opportunity have passed unused, and Senate disagreements about child tax credit linkage have complicated things further.
